original_question:
Which statement is sarcastic?
Options:
(A) Thank you stranger on the internet, I won't trust your three word post without any sources over someone offering solid advice claiming to actually be from the country in question
(B) Thank you stranger on the internet, I will trust your three word post without any sources over someone offering solid advice claiming to actually be from the country in question
subquestion: Write a scenario involving the sunk cost fallacy that occurs during a group project. Classify the scenario as an instance of the sunk cost fallacy.
answer: The scenario where a group of students continues a project despite having better options because they believe they cannot waste the money and time already invested is an instance of the sunk cost fallacy.
judge_answer:
The scenario described fits the definition of the sunk cost fallacy. Sunk costs are costs that have already been incurred and cannot be recovered. In this case, the students continue with the project because they feel they cannot "waste" the time and resources they've already put into it, even though continuing might not be the best course of action moving forward. This is a classic example of the sunk cost fallacy.

FINAL ANSWER: correct
